keyTerms.House of Lancaster: One branch of the Plantagenet royal family, symbolized by the red rose, whose claim to the throne was weakened by Henry VI's madness
keyTerms.House of York: The rival Plantagenet branch, symbolized by the white rose, led by Richard Duke of York and later his sons
keyTerms.Battle of Bosworth Field: The 1485 battle where Henry Tudor defeated Richard III, ending the Wars of the Roses and launching the Tudor dynasty
keyTerms.Tudor Rose: The red-and-white merged symbol created by Henry VII's marriage to Elizabeth of York, representing the union of the warring houses
keyTerms.Princes in the Tower: The two young sons of Edward IV, imprisoned and almost certainly murdered by their uncle Richard III in the Tower of London
